assessment_items.question_content:
Multiplying $3\times8$ is like having $3$ rows of dots with $8$ dots in each row:

![A rectangular array with 3 rows and 8 dots per row](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/dc1f80d17a36e3f8143d813354b53ac5290d9c66)

**How many dots are there?**

$3\times8=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
There are $\blueD3$ rows with $\purpleC8$ dots in each row.

![The same array with the rows numbered 1 through 3 and the dots in the row numbered 1 through 8](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/68bde365328929dc29aca231997af747ecc4552d)

We can find the total number of dots by adding $\purpleC8$ dots $\blueD3$ times:

![The same array with each row labeled, 8 dots](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/04969d47450b89e256a46f6f60fd24997cbd0018)

$\purpleC8 + \purpleC8 + \purpleC8  = {}?$

![The same array with the dots numbered 1 through 24](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e503ec97014260d7ec60f52a562efffe36cfa090)

$\purpleC8 + \purpleC8 + \purpleC8  = \tealD{24}$

There are $\tealD{24}$ dots.

$3 \times 8 = \tealD{24}$

assessment_items.question_content:
Multiplying $4\times7$ is like having $4$ rows of dots with $7$ dots in each row:

[[☃ image 1]]

**How many dots are there?**

$4\times7=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
There are $\blueD4$ rows with $\purpleC7$ dots in each row.

[[☃ image 1]]

We can find the total number of dots by adding $\purpleC7$ dots $\blueD4$ times:

[[☃ image 1]]

$\purpleC7 + \purpleC7 + \purpleC7 + \purpleC7 = {}?$

[[☃ image 1]]

$\purpleC7 + \purpleC7 + \purpleC7 + \purpleC7  = \tealD{28}$

There are $\tealD{28}$ dots.

$4 \times 7 = \tealD{28}$

options.alt: An array of circles arranged in 4 rows of 7 circles each.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the visual to complete the equation.**

$3 \times ~$[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $=~ 18$



[[☃ image 1]]
hints:
We can relate the drawing to multiplication:

$\blueD{\text{ Number of rows}}\times\greenD{\text{ Number of objects in each row}}=\purpleC{\text{ Total}}$

So, in $\blueD3\times$ ___ = $\purpleC{18}$, we know there are $\blueD3$ rows and $\purpleC{18}$ total dots. 

How many dots are in each row?

There are $\greenD6$ dots in each row. 



[[☃ image 1]]
$\blueD3\times\greenD6{}=\purpleC{18}$

options.alt: An array of 18 dots arranged in 3 rows of 6 dots each. A line separates each row.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the array to complete the equation.**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\times7= 14$




[[☃ image 1]]
hints:
We can relate the drawing to multiplication:

$\blueD{\text{ Number of rows}}\times\purpleC{\text{ Number of objects in each row}}=\greenD{\text{ Total}}$

So, in ___$\times$ $\purpleC7= \greenD{14}$, we know there are $\purpleC7$ dots in each row and $\greenD{14}$ total dots. 



[[☃ image 1]]

How many rows of $\purpleC7$ dots do we have?

There are $\blueD2$ rows of dots with $\purpleC7$ dots in each row:



[[☃ image 1]]

$\purpleC7 + \purpleC7 = \greenD{14}$
$\blueD2 \times \purpleC7 = \greenD{14}$

options.alt: An array of 14 dots arranged in 2 rows of 7 dots each. A line separates the rows.

assessment_items.question_content:
There are $3$ rows of cheeseburgers, with $5$ cheeseburgers in each row.  


 **How many cheeseburgers are there in all?**



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We can relate the drawing to multiplication:

$\blueD{\text{ Number of rows}}\times\purpleC{\text{ Number of objects in each row}}=\greenD{\text{ Total}}$

So, there are $\blueD3$ rows of cheeseburgers with $\purpleC5$ cheeseburgers in each row.



[[☃ image 1]]

We can find the total number of cheeseburgers by adding $\purpleC5$ cheeseburgers $\blueD3$ times:



[[☃ image 1]]



$\blueD3 \times\purpleC5 =\purpleC5 +\purpleC5 +\purpleC5  = \greenD{15}$

There are $\greenD{15}$ cheeseburgers in all.

$\blueD3 \times\purpleC5 = \greenD{15}$

options.alt: An array of cheeseburgers arranged in 3 rows of 5 cheeseburgers each.

assessment_items.question_content:
There are $2$ rows of sharks, with $9$ sharks in each row.  


 **How many sharks are there in all?**



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We can relate the drawing to multiplication:

$\blueD{\text{ Number of rows}}\times\purpleC{\text{ Number of objects in each row}}=\greenD{\text{ Total}}$

So, there are $\blueD2$ rows of sharks with $\purpleC9$ sharks in each row.



[[☃ image 1]]

We can find the total number of sharks by adding $\purpleC9$ sharks $\blueD2$ times:



[[☃ image 1]]



$\blueD2 \times \purpleC9 =\purpleC9 +\purpleC9  = \greenD{18}$

There are $\greenD{18}$ sharks in all.

$\blueD2 \times \purpleC9 = \greenD{18}$

options.alt: An array of sharks arranged in 2 rows of 9 sharks each.

assessment_items.question_content:
There are $7$ rows of cheese wedges, with $8$ wedges in each row.  


 **How many cheese wedges are there in all?**



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We can relate the drawing to multiplication:

$\blueD{\text{ Number of rows}}\times\purpleC{\text{ Number of objects in each row}}=\greenD{\text{ Total}}$

So, there are $\blueD7$ rows of cheese wedges with $\purpleC8$ wedges in each row.



[[☃ image 1]]

We can find the total number of cheese wedges by adding $\purpleC8$ wedges $\blueD7$ times:



[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD7 \times \purpleC8 =\purpleC8 +\purpleC8 +\purpleC8 + \purpleC8 +\purpleC8 +\purpleC8 + \purpleC8  = \greenD{56}$

There are $\greenD{56}$ cheese wedges in all.

$\blueD7 \times \purpleC8 = \greenD{56}$

options.alt: An array of cheese wedges arranged in 7 rows of 8 cheese wedges each.

assessment_items.question_content:
There are $4$ rows of acorns, with $7$ acorns in each row. 

**How many acorns are there in all?**



[[☃ image 1]]



[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We can relate the drawing to multiplication:

$\blueD{\text{ Number of rows}}\times\purpleC{\text{ Number of objects in each row}}=\greenD{\text{ Total}}$

So, there are $\blueD4$ rows of acorns with $\purpleC7$ acorns in each row.



[[☃ image 1]]

We can find the total number of acorns by adding $\purpleC7$ acorns $\blueD4$ times:



[[☃ image 1]]



$\blueD4 \times \purpleC7 = \purpleC7 +\purpleC7 +\purpleC7 + \purpleC7  = \greenD{28}$

There are $\greenD{28}$ acorns in all.

$\blueD4 \times \purpleC7 = \greenD{28}$

options.alt: An array of acorns arranged in 4 rows of 7 acorns each.

assessment_items.question_content:
There are $6$ rows of bananas, with $10$ bananas in each row. 



[[☃ image 1]]

**How many bananas are there in all?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We can relate the drawing to multiplication:

$\blueD{\text{ Number of rows}}\times\purpleC{\text{ Number of objects in each row}}=\greenD{\text{ Total}}$

So, there are $\blueD{6}$ rows of bananas with $\purpleC{10}$ bananas in each row.



[[☃ image 1]]

We can find the total number of bananas by adding $\purpleC{10}$ bananas $\blueD{6}$ times:



[[☃ image 1]]



$\blueD6 \times \purpleC{10} =  \purpleC{10} +\purpleC{10} +\purpleC{10} + \purpleC{10} + \purpleC{10} +\purpleC{10}  = \greenD{60}$

There are $\greenD{60}$ bananas in all.

$\blueD6 \times \purpleC{10} = \greenD{60}$

options.alt: An array of bananas arranged in 6 rows of 10 bananas each.
